channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 131 replies — 160.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 131 • Updated: 2026-05-29 12:04 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283041
>>62272050
Hmmm.. what do you mean?

62283050
>>62282849
That’s why people should wait for the actual TBV launch first instead of screaming revolution or scam this early.
At least the Aave v4 governance process is already moving now.

62283055
>>62272643
At least check it sometimes bro. You might already be retired and just don’t know it yet.

62283065
>>62272208
A 2x or 3x from here would still outperform most traditional investments by a huge margin.

62283375
>>62283050
Defi hacks are no joke

62283377
>>62282845
Why are you flexing your imagination?

62283380
>>62271712
Money

62283769
>>62276880
ARMA hasn't passed yet, but there's already a Reserve.

62283901
THE BIG DUMPING
>every cycle 90-70% loss. why would it be this time different.

62283926
>>62283901
diminishing returns mean diminishing drawbacks, retard

62283940
>>62282847
I hope that big brain stuck around, for my sake

62283952
>>62271712
Bitcoin is a pyramid scheme and a waste of electricity. I was an early adopter of bitcoin fully thinking it was a scam and I sold mine in 2016 after I had enough to retire. I understand the whole point about fiat currency but what I think we need to do is just get rid of money all together and use assets as money like stocks and commodities. Money was invented so people didn't have to carry their assets around but know we all walk around with computers in our pocket that would allow us to trade our assets like how things used to be. I should be able to go to McDonalds and buy my food with corn for example. Bitcoin only exists for countries to bypass sanctions and if you buy bitcoin you are just giving more money to Putin and all the other scammers and terrorists.

62285517
>>62272225
They've got their preferences

62285578
>>62283375
Yeah, DeFi hacks are getting pretty crazy lately. That’s why multiple audits, formal verification, penetration testing, and cryptography reviews matter much more now with this much capital involved.

62285585
>>62272647
You'll regret your choices lol

62285589
>>62272667
You think you're smart?

62286335
>>62271712
No money, no job
They literally won't even take me at minimum wage places because I don't speak spanish.
So, no way to generate the income to buy BTC in the first place.

62287162
>>62274011
Work for it anon

62287177
>>62283952
You can already by McDonalds with corn. Use credit card and pay off credit card with corn futures. The problem is your dumbass is bleeding wealth by keeping your net worth in corn (or companies, or anything besides bitcoin) because people are going to make more corn (and ever faster each year due to tech advances) and inflate away your wealth.

62287383
>>62285578
but why remains the hack

62287392
>>62286335
If you're married at don't have money, you deserve a divorce

62287393
>>62271712
It's a gamble and I only bet on sure things. In fact I like to have my profits guaranteed under contract and insured BEFORE I spend a dime of my own money. I always sell BEFORE I buy so I know exactly how much I'm making in advance.

62287464
Most of the retards have been bled dry. BTC only exists now as a system for whales to lead exit pumps and bleed the occasional new entrants.

62287628
>>62287383
Most of the biggest DeFi disasters over the years involved bridges, custodians, or avoidable trust assumptions somewhere in the stack. That's exactly why security architecture gets attention now.

62287630
>>62280441
You're thinking the wrong way

62287632
>>62282426
Well, depends on them

62287634
BTC has just tracked the nasdaq for years until recently and now it just sucks ass no matter what happens.

Hype has peaked. Most of it is owned by boomers now and its not a hedge against anything it just dumps the hardest on bad news until boomers, banks and third worlders get tired of bagholding and it goes sub 20k forever

62287690
>>62287634
So you're saying btc is only a big hell hype?

62288099
>>62287634
nonsense

62288112
>>62273994
So it's not worth it?

item.title: Question for oldfags about vidya prices: — 31 replies — 124.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Question for oldfags about vidya prices:

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-05-29 12:48 UTC

OP

i looked into the price of SNES games as well as master system back in the days:

-average snes game sold for 50$ in the early 9às, inflation adjusted that's 120$ today
Some sold higher than that : donkey kong came for 70$, so 150$ today

-30 to 60$ for sega master system, but it was the late 80s so like 130$ today.

Why are gooners crying about the price of their vidyas? Things used to be way more expensive for a 2 d platformer that would give you max 100 hours of play if not half. Also today's collector price for most of those games is still LOWER than what people paid for when it came out brand new back in the days, so still no profit after holding for 30+ years kek

Early replies (first 3)

62283131
>>62283119
There was a time not so long ago when you could hardly give that shit away. Nobody wanted to play old 2D games when there was amazing new stuff coming out that you couldn't have even imagined a few years prior.

62283133
>>62283119
Oldfag here, they’re crying because all these games were $1 at your local flea market in 2005, AND THEY DIDN’T EVEN WANT TO BUY. It’s even worse than missing bitcoin because it was right under their nose, they saw, they knew, and they shrugged it off.

62283135
>>62283131
snes games were cheap like 10 + years ago. Master system is still very cheap, but nobody gives a shit. I guess rn the money is in xbox / game cube games

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283136
Also, thread's theme:

https://youtu.be/E5_DDRkH70E?t=458

62284191
So as a former soiboi with a massive collection of everything from
>atari
>nes
>snes
>genesis/32x
>n64
>gc
>ps1
>dc
What am I in for and also when/how do I sell to get max value for all this shit that tbqhfam I'm just as comfy emulating? Good crt monitors are like digging for dinosaur bones and composite input screens are like asking about where the bluray players are these days. This shit is collecting dust and I've got projects that need funding

62284329
>>62283119
Back then the only markets were NA, SA, EU and JP, and there were fewer people in those markets. Also it was mostly for kids, while now it's for kids + teens + adults + grandmas, and both genders. Also now China and India have entered the market, so there are a lot more consumers. This allows developers to split the software cost on a lot more consumers, and the production of the hardware became cheaper as well, and online digital distribution basically killed cartridges and discs. The cartridges used to cost a lot.

62284510
>>62284329
>Back then the only markets were NA, SA, EU and JP
We didn't know how good we had it. Aryan markets only. No surprise it all went downhill.

>>62284191
well, some retards actually bought the old game to play them, and the price dropped when they were reedited, like final fantasy 7, got myself a copy for 30$ after the remake. Before that it was 100$.
But, as an amateur of fine things, i have to say there is something unique about playing on the exact hardware of the time.

I don't expect that much of a growth for retro video games because of that. Also the hardware self destruct, you have to be able repair and maitain it and some chips can't be bought back.
However, some stuff is rare and will get only get more scarce so it could increase exponentially this decade.

62284586
You people are so fucking stupid. Its the cartridge. Compared to the 20th century memory and manufacturing is dirt cheap.
People don't want 80$ video games because its genuinely ripping them off. Add in that a majority of game sales are digital now and therefore have lower overhead for distribution costs, and you're really paying that extra 20$ for DEI consulting, infinity third world outsourcing for cutscenes, and to pay one jillion middle managers salaries.
Bundle that with a corporate structure that milks people dry with microtransactions and treating customers like "consumers" and people won't fucking buy.

62284597
>>62284586
nigger, i played 1K hours of counter strike and bought the game 10 years ago for 8 eurobuxxes. Do you think you could get the same value from a 130$ 2 d platformers finished in 30 hours?

62284605
>>62284597
Thats the final factor. Collectible value. Physical copies are tangible and worth their value in resale.

62284628
>>62284605
agreed. But i still find that those snes games and other old gen platforms were extremely expensive. Obviously at the time it was the best video games available so people were happy to pay but that's still very expensive, especially if you play some sega master system who aged so poorly you can't even be bother to play them 2 hours. 130++$ for a game like that was super expensive.

62284656
>>62284628
I just explained it you dumb faggot. READ

62284666
>>62284656
you didn't explain shit, it was very expensive, a bunch of plastic and one mass produced circuit board isn't worth that much

62284681
>>62284666
0 iq mongrel. Kys

62284717
>>62284681
same fagging phone poster, opinion discarded

62284778
>>62283119
I think you'd just rent most of your games from a video store and then get like one or two for Christmas. Also people would share games with their friends because they weren't socially-handicapped tiktok scrollers.

62285855
>>62284778
rent games? so lame. did anyone actually do that? you buy and resell second hand, game cost you 0 basically that's what i used to do with my ps2

62286204
>>62284628
>at the time it was the best video games available so people
Home video games sucked compared to arcade games and everybody knew it, unless you had a Neo Geo (but each game was like $700 in today’s money)

62286216
>>62286204
Street Fighter II for SNES was amazing despite not being arcade where some nignog could chimp out after getting assblasted by Blanka

62286230
>>62285855
>rent games? so lame.
Less so than selling your own games.

>did anyone actually do that?
Yes and it was amazing, especially when renting those esoteric JP games and playing them with an adapter. It was like temporarily unlocking a new world, you were inviting all your friends to explore these games to the fullest for the weekend.

Of course a lonely consoomer from 2026 can’t get it.

62286236
>>62286216
It was very enjoyable indeed, but still not as good as the arcade version.

62286393
>>62283119
My most sacred memory is when my mom picked me up from 1st grade in 1987 and as we are driving home she told me that she got me a new video game where you can SAVE the game and start from there when you come back to it. WOW.

62286541
>>62283119
When I got my first PC in 1997 at 14 I rarely bought games after that. I had consoles but I didn't have money to buy games and my parents wouldn't just buy me any game I wanted whenever.

62286565
>>62283119
They're bitching just to bitch. There's also like 1000 ways to emulate those games and play them like the originals or better, so really they're retardedly nostalgic to have THE cartridge and then mad a bunch of other people are too

62286669
>>62283133
Nobody is talking about used video games. They're crying because modern consoles are approaching a thousand bucks and moden games are heading towards 100. But anon correctly noted that back in the day, it was $50-60 for a new NES cartridge or a new computer game on three 360kb diskettes, and at the time minimum wage was something like $4 an hour. Adjusted for inflation, a game like Super Mario that you could beat in an hour, or Kings Quest that lasts you a couple of hours max, cost $161.

62287647
>>62283131
>There was a time not so long ago when you could hardly give that shit away. Nobody wanted to play old 2D games when there was amazing new stuff coming out that you couldn't have even imagined a few years prior.
This
I bought a movie rental stores stock for peanuts when they were going broke in 2012, walked in and offered 2000$ cash for the lot (about 5000 DVDs and blu rays and 2500 PS1, PS2 and 360 games). It was a niche movie buff DVD and game store. Have a pretty complete movie and game library and enjoy it a lot to this day. I am randy marsh.

62287668
>>62286669
>Nobody is talking about used video games.
OP was literally talking about "today's collector price for most of those games" but okay

62287673
>>62287668
both can come into discussion, but the idea was that game used to be even more expensive than they are today yet people bitch about their free world 10k hours lifetime game selling for half what a super nintendo game used to sell

62287727
>>62283119
I don't know what a SNES is (Nintendo consoles are for faggots), but I used to buy pirated PS1/PS2 games for $5 from some shady dude back in the day. Even back then, only retards would pay $50 for a video game. I see times haven't changed...

62288182
>>62287668
Yes.
>today's colllector price is still LOWER than what people paid for when it came out brand new
He's still talking about how prices today are still lower when accounting for inflation, even for used collector games, compared to the price for games back in the day.
And he's right. A nintendo entertainment system with one game (Mario/Duck Hunt) adjusted for inflation cost $450.
A New 386 16 with 1mb of ram and a 20mb hard drive with 16 color graphics and midi sound card cost $8500, adjusted for inflation that's $25,000.
People bitching about prices for games these days really have no idea. Their idea of what things cost back in the day is skewed by a combination television where the latest technology was placed "in the common home" as a deliberate advertisement and a long run of (relatively) dramatically discounted prices from the late 90s until the late 10s.
Even today their expectations are ridiculous: "It shouldn't cost as much as when you had physical media because you can just download it!", like servers and connections that can provide fast 85gb video game downloads to millions of people around the world are not expensive themselves.

62288229
>>62283119
People's real wages were also higher.

item.title: ITT: Post an image that sums up /biz/ succinctly — 22 replies — 88.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ITT: Post an image that sums up /biz/ succinctly

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-05-29 12:44 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62285243
>>62285128
crazy the amount of time people spend fudding a random alt coin.
it happens so much, the amount of energy that's spent on it buy a group of individuals, or a bot? either way, it's very interesting from the outside
made me start to think the linkies are onto something..
to anyone else on the outside, like anyone here from pmg or smg? how does this look? pretty crazy to me..
anyway the constant fud against link made me think i should secure a suicide bag of it, and now i just buy 1 link everytime someone posts a fud thread for free, in their own spare time.. which is crazy to me
so must be trying to hide something

62285245
quick, make more threads!

62285257
>>62285128
so true, king

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285535
>>62285243
>i just buy 1 link everytime someone posts a fud thread for free, in their own spare time.. which is crazy to me
STFU nigga you aint doin that dont lie fees would rape u

62285592
>>62285535
like 1-2 cent a buy?

62285749
>>62285128
>chainlink staking mechanism colorized

62285799
>>62285128
Ikyk

62285888
>>62285243
Link monkeys are Lolcows. DUH.

62287261
>>62285888
anymore then bbby or gamestop guys? yet you don't see 20 threads fudding or shitting on them everyday?
it's pretty weird..
i have less then 500 link btw, at average of 7-8, i literally don't care if it moons or not, just one of many "if the schizos are right' small bags
plus we all learned link triggers you transgenic, homos so much, hence my continued dca, despite knowing nothing about chainlink or dtcc or orcale (thought that was a different company)

62287574
>>62285243
From my perspective, all crypto is a scam unless its called FARTCOIN

62287615
>>62287261

>I'm DCAing something I don't even understand that is dieing because it "owns the transgenic"

lmao what a fucking retard

62287648
>>62287615
retards retard
retards win

62287707
>>62285243
nah it's just a natural reaction to the link shill spam that's been going on since 2017, but you know this since you are a shill

62287709
>>62287615
>dieing
I haven't owned Link since 2020 but I might just buy some after reading your post

62287712
Very effective link thread. I'm even tempted to buy it, even though I don't do crypto.

62287714
>>62285535
What is a percentage

62287716
>>62287712
>"Hello fellow huwhite saars, by vishnu i think we should all redeem our apple gift cards and buy link tokens saars, berry good coin saar"

62287729
>>62287261
Both of them keep it to a general. Linkfaggots, in contrast, spammed /biz/ for years.

62287741
>>62285243
you're a mentally retarded cuckold LARPing as a non LINK holder. Chainlink is trading at a lower price than 6 years ago

62288220
>>62287729
>>62287707
>spam up the board as a response to link spam
genuis
>>62287741
i have a little link because of the constant schizo war between fudders and shills, just makes me think i should have a little in case
I never even told anyone to buy link in this thread, link isn't even 1 percent of my port.

item.title: is this chart FUD — 18 replies — 44.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
is this chart FUD

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-29 12:29 UTC

OP

?

Early replies (first 3)

62287983
>>62287974
No it just shows 7 years of suppression

62287986
>>62287974
looks bullish 2 me

62287991
>>62287983
Vechain is getting suppressed too. Wake up sheeple!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62287997
>>62287991
2021 bull run is part of LINK suppression, they pumped every single dogshit coin while running LINK sideways

62288000
>>62287997
so why did your shitcoin miss the 2024-2025 bullrun? who's fault was it?

62288062
>>62288000
What do you mean? The whole market crashed moment LINK hit $30 and we have been in bear ever since

62288072
>>62288062
can't wait to see mentally ill baggies like you miss another bullrun in 2028

62288085
>>62288072
I wish i was mentally ill but sadly suppression is very real

Simply open up the chart

62288093
>>62288085
what if they suppress it so hard it goes to zero and dies?

62288100
>>62288093
If they wanted that LINK would be at 0 already

62288103
>>62288100
who's "they" you mentally ill baggie?

62288107
>>62288103
Its impossible to tell really

Could be CLL, Binance, banks, blackrock, glowies

Maybe mix of all of them

62288154
>>62288107
Ka-BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM
Baggie faggie

62288157
>>62287991
It's the same cope for all 2018 alts. I was looking at an ADA group the other day and they're talking about how they're artificially range bound for the past 6 months and every time they reach 30 cents or something BTC dumps. It's just inane cope from people you wouldn't respect if you could see their face.

62288164
>>62288157
Im sure those ADA baggies have screenshots of BTC dumping on every ADA resistance lvl & news (if they have had any)

62288168
just say it stinkies
say you LOST lmao

62288173
>>62288164
They probably have folders full. I don't even know anything about ADA to know whether it's shit or not but it just becomes clear there's just the same low intelligence baggies recirculating copes in any investment community. It's not even crypto exclusive.

I don't know what to advise from it as their presence doesn't mean the project is definitely shit but I guess it's a sign it's sputtering.

62288176
>>62288173
Id like to see those folders because no one has been able such proofs on /biz/ during these 9 years

item.title: I have a feeling ETH will rise back — 10 replies — 39.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
I have a feeling ETH will rise back

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-05-29 12:38 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62286999
Standard chartered says ETH to hit $40k

62287001
>>62286999
4.8 trillion market cap? Really nigga?

62287010
>>62286995
From $2.5k down to $1.5k over and over again and you’re still broke. How?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62287015
>>62286999
>>62287001
since bitcoin is "digital gold" I assume they just looked up the market cap of silver to make this prediction

62287019
>>62287001
>Distributed computation and storage with active developments in homomorphic encryption is not worth 4.8 trillions dollars.
Why are there so many retards here?

62287186
>>62286995
cope

62288056
>>62286995
I told you to sell in august. Not that i did sell all but a lot.

62288098
>>62286999
Standard Chartered just made up a bunch of numbers and said "this is our forecast" lol

Also checked

62288128
>>62287019
>homomorphic encryption
sounds gay as fuck, no wonder your shitcoin is trading at the same price as 2021

62288202
>>62288128
It's closed source computation.
It's current value is reflective of the ignorance of the network's capabilities.
Can you explain how the Ethereum network works? Save your fingers. You've already demonstrated that you can't.

item.title: how do you avoid your infos being leaked ans the subsequent home jacking? — 20 replies — 36.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
how do you avoid your infos being leaked ans the subsequent home jacking?

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-05-29 12:20 UTC

OP

France has seen an explosion in crypto kidnapping. This explosion came after the state demanded citizens to declare their "self hosted" wallets and their balances. There also was a data leak from some kind of private investment company after a hack.
People with balances as low as 2 btc have been targetted and successfully forced to give up all their assets under threats. Some people living abroad have had their relatives kidnapped.

How do you avoid this shit? all the regulations on crypto have been an absolute disaster, the only end goal is to steal your wealth via taxation and state control. This totally ruins the whole crypto thing. There is no point in holding large amounts of cryptos if it puts a target on your back and on your familly and you can't sleep well at night.
Now many anons might think this is a french thing but eventually more leaks will happen and nobody will be safe. there will be lists with your name and address and all the purchase you did in the last years, even maybe your depo address that criminals will be able to track and see what most likely happened with your balance.

One simple mitigation measure would be to use many exchanges, many adresses, as to make it appear you hold less, but this is also an annoying process that require strict management and doesn't guarantee additional leaks at the state level if they group all your trading infos.
You should stop using any KYC, stop paying taxes on government that don't care at all about your safety, and go full down the crypto ecosystem giving up off ramps as much as you can.

Early replies (first 3)

62287742
Generic India hate thread disguised as something else.

62287753
>>62287731
>France
That's what you get for living in Africa.

62287759
>>62287753
while i can't disagree, the problem remains. if you think living in the usa or germany makes you safe from this issue you're delulu. Usa is known for its violent crime just wait til a list of easy 1 Mil++ robberies on individual citizens start going around and see what happens

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62287821
>>62287759
>if you think living in the usa or germany makes you safe from this issue you're delulu.
well, those places are also shitholes, but they don't "demanded citizens to declare their "self hosted" wallets and their balances"
kill retarded communists

62287831
>>62287753
agreed, the french deserve 100% of what they get, they STILL advocate for communism and whine about the 'rich' not paying their share, even though the vast majority of their rich fled that shithole years ago.

62287835
>>62287731
Dont live in 3rd world countries like france and obviously most kidnappings happen due to money flex not leaks

62287837
the leftists do this. The other half doesn't but get jewed constantly.
anyway that's a stupid argument

62287850
>>62287753
France voted for it, they deserve all they get and more.

62287859
>>62287850
>>62287821
>>62287831

While your answers are completely selfish and retarded, i took a visit to rubic and rubic actually allows cross chain swaps between bitcoin and many others stables so that's one way you can do crypto to crypto trade without KYC, and sell high to rebuy lower without every retard gov agency being aware of it.
I'm there are a lot of solutions out there.

62287875
>>62287731
the gov home address leaks are the insane part of this coz you cant really prevent that without not reporting (which is kinda impossible if you ever want to spend a decent amount)

only way to prevent i guess is having a holiday home and just pointing to that

62287892
>>62287821
Database leaks happen all the time. Its just a matter of time before a KYC exchange gets breached and your info gets leaked

62287918
>>62287731
>vidrel
Poor wagie, it sucks that everything is filmed now. He can't forget this even if I bet he wants to.

62287930
>>62287731
>This explosion came after the state demanded citizens to declare their "self hosted" wallets and their balances
All by design

62287931
>>62287731
>I have a lot of money in crypto/cash/gold
>Omg why am I a target for robbery?!
What did you expect, faggot? Life like this. It's the way it is

62287985
>>62287731
1. lie
2. do not ever disclose your balance
3. have 2 homes in case

62288016
>>62287931
very forward thinking anon.
Difference is with a bank account it's much more anonymous and you can't just get robbed in a wrench key attack.

62288126
>>62287892
>KYC exchange
retard. THE FUCKING STATE IS MAKING YOU DO IT

62288135
>>62287985
>GOY ID
Kek has spoken.

62288136
>>62287759
>easy robberies
Nigga I will kill anyone who comes around trying to rob me. I am armed and insane.

62288150
>>62288136
Yeah, imagine giving up your seed phrase to some angry African because it has a gun that it barely knows how to use.

item.title: Is BTC unironically gonna moon with all this 55k talk? — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is BTC unironically gonna moon with all this 55k talk?

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-29 12:45 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62288091
>>62288068
Yep, after this bear market it’s going to be 1 BTC = 2 BTC

62288130
>>62288068
FOOD PEPE RUGGED

62288153
40k October

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62288160
>>62288068
Going to fall further and further, the crypto meme is over

62288170
>>62288068
Has Baron slurped enouh yet?
Also the bullish policy should released before midterm.

62288178
>>62288068
Yes.
The biggest corporation in the world is about to begin buying.
Bottomtober fags will buy back in this Summer.

62288199
>>62288068
You would think so but it may take time
>>62288130
NO

62288223
>>62288068
I fucking typed 55k here once and now this is the mainstream target of biz? Gg 15k incoming....

item.title: CAN CEO'S PERPETUALLY PAY THEMSELVES A HIGH SALARY WHILE GOING INTO DEBT SPIRAL? — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
CAN CEO'S PERPETUALLY PAY THEMSELVES A HIGH SALARY WHILE GOING INTO DEBT SPIRAL?

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-05-29 12:41 UTC

OP

worthless lowly poor retarded chud here, claude told me a normie answer that i dont trust, so im asking here


Can CEO's of medium sized businesses more or less leech off of a business by repeatedly taking out loans and covering previous loans with new loans until the entire business goes bankrupt from debt spiral meanwhile extracting a 1-5 million annual salary? This question assumes they went to harvard or some high status school and that they were able to weasel their way into the position to begin with, maybe through a consulting gig or a venture capital connection or some shit like that.

Basically, what I'm asking is, what is stopping ceo's from this sorta gravy train assuming they cover their tracks legally (as in they do everything by the book in a maliciously compliant way)?

At what scale does this occur?

Early replies (first 3)

62287045
>>62287038
>what is stopping ceo's from this sorta gravy train
nothing really. and this is scratching the surface of the shady shit they do. it's all a grift from top to bottom.

62288122
>>62287038
CEO's don't decide their salary, the board of directors does that.

62288197
>>62287038
>At what scale does this occur?
>he doesn't know the entire economy operates like this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62288214
>>62287038
executives are wagies just like the rest of us. eventually the board of directors would fire them. hf the BOD doesn't do this, the shareholders might raise a lawsuit against the company. if the executives and BOD aren't acting in the best interests of the shareholders, they could be held personally liable.

item.title: IT ALWAYS GOES UP! Bobo is temporary, Mumu is forever! — 54 replies — 14.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
IT ALWAYS GOES UP! Bobo is temporary, Mumu is forever!

Open thread • Replies: 54 • Updated: 2026-05-29 09:15 UTC

OP

IT ALWAYS GOES UP!
Bobo is temporary, Mumu is forever!

Early replies (first 3)

62283021
>>62283018
>please buy my overpriced bags before the market crashes

62283022
>please buy my overpriced bags before the market crashes

62283030
>please buy my overpriced bags before the market crashes

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62284284
>>62283018
>just wait like 5 years goy, don't sell, it'll return to green eventually

62284502
>>62284284
>the twin towers of anon's portfolio

62284646
just buy and hold for 45 years, that's my motto

62284879
>>62284502
kek and they're right next to "september 11", I swear it was unintentional

62284961
>>62284646
>DUDE JUST WORK AND DEAL IN THE REAL WORLD FOR 45 YEARS

do goyim really?

62285025
>>62283021
>>62284284
>>62284646
whats the alternative?
>inb4 just buy gold

62286115
>>62284502
Kek

>>62285025
The market can keep on being exuberant for many months, so keep more and more cash by your side, a play that’s too contrarian like leveraged shorts could wipe you

62286525
>>62283018
>IT ALWAYS GOES UP!
Until it doesn't.

62286553
>>62283258
>>Your current holdings get rekt by 90%
That never happened though. And you can just hodl and it usually bounces back within. A few months like OP's chart shows.

62286561
Wagmii

62286596
>>62283018
>IT ALWAYS GOES UP!
Except for where it goes down

62286638
>>62286525
Literally the best time to buy though. How do you think gains are made.

62286688
>>62286638
Boughted at peak - fucked for 25 years

62286712
>>62286688
Wow you only ever got income once in 25 years and it corresponded to a market peak? What a wild life you've lived.

62286756
>Wow you only ever sold your house once in 25 years?
>Wow you only ever inherited once in 25 years?

62286769
>put brexit on the graph like england actually matters
holy delusional little broski wilted flower

62286806
>>62286688
Investing in 1935 looks comfy. 32 and 33 you had to perfectly time it to catch the bottom.

62286847
>>62286688
The Nikkei 225 is ever better, 35 years before recovering if you bought the top in 1989

62286861
Listen folks, diversify, you want at least 3 different stocks

62286914
>>62286596
If assumed to be linear we still have a ways to go before we reach the peak

62286919
>>62286756
You've never heard of DCA and you're being smug about it

62286996
>>62286919
But Vanguard research taught us that:

>Lump-sum investment strategies beat common cost averaging investment
strategies two-thirds of the time, according to historical and simulated
market data

This is science, you must go all in with all your funds immediately, just unplug your brain and buy the top!!!

62287000
>>62286996
Sorry for botched greentext lol

62287020
>>62285025

62287086
>>62283018
check the Nikkei 225

62287105
>>62283018
>just pick the right ones at all times

62287638
>>62283030
this

62287643
What a bullshit graph. SPY went down like 50% during The Great Recession. This makes it look like it went down 8% lol.

62287646
>>62286553

small caps have had 90%+ drawdowns many times. most companies fail over time.

DCA index funds works. DCA shitty company = lose your house

62287732
>>62286553
If you want to be pedantic the percentage might be 80 something percent instead, the point still stands.
OPs chart is misleading in a number of ways, first and foremost because it has been squeezed on the X axis so that a block of 20 fucking years looks like nothing, do that to many charts and they will look great and smoothes out those "dips".
The Y axis is presumably % gain, if you are rekt for 80%, you need a 400% gain to get back to that level, at 10% YoY that's 16 years to recover those crashed assets.
Yes you are buying more in the meantime, and those assets are increasing in value 10% YoY, fantastic. Everything you held before stays wrecked for years and the cope is to factor it all in together and take an average.

62286979
>>62286974

62286993
buying the amazon dip tomorrow

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62287004
We have to keep relearning the Challenger disaster I guess.

62287005
elon was in the replies gloating, what a cunt

62287007
i guess more layoffs coming then

62287014
>>62287005
>NOOOOOO DON'T MAKE FUN OF MY HECKIN ROCKET! NOOOOOOOOOO

62287024
Manay ominous signs this past week

62287030
>>62286974

62287042
>>62286974
Musk must be laughing his ass off

62287046
>>62287007
Either this or emergency unpaid overtime shifts.

62287049
KWAB

62287052
CLEAN UP THE DEBRIS, WAGIE!!!

62287125
>>62286974
bezos can suck the shit of my asshole

62287128
>>62286974
So? Eill stonks go down and crypto up now? No? So?

62287260
>>62286974
AMAZON x XRP PARTNERSHIP?????????

BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62287271
>>62287052
what an ugly freak god damn nigga even with 270 billion dollars being this ugly has got to be rough.

62287278
Gender studies be one of them classes where you realize society really out here making random rules then acting like them rules dropped from the sky . The whole subject talks about how people get treated different based on gender, race, class, and identity. It ain’t just about “men versus women.” Nah, it’s deeper than that. It’s about power, who got it, and who keeps the system running like a broken carnival ride .

Back in the day, society pushed these roles heavy. Dudes supposed to be tough, work jobs, never cry. Girls supposed to stay pretty, quiet, cook food, and smile through suffering like customer service employees on minimum wage . Gender studies basically walks into the room and says, “Yo, who invented these goofy rules?” That’s where people start talking about gender as a social construct. Meaning society made a lot of these expectations up over time and everybody just downloaded the update without reading the terms and conditions .

Another big thing in gender studies is intersectionality. Big word, simple meaning. Different struggles stack together like bad Wi-Fi connections. Somebody can face problems because of gender, race, money, or all three at once. The system don’t hit everybody equally. Some people start life on easy mode while others spawn into expert difficulty with no tutorial .

Media also catches strays in this class because movies, ads, music, and social media keep pushing stereotypes 24/7. Folks grow up seeing the same roles repeated until they think it’s “normal.” Gender studies tries to challenge those dominant narratives and make people question why society moves the way it does.

At the end of the day, the class teaches that identity, power, and culture all connected. Also teaches students how capitalism be profiting off stereotypes while everybody else just trying to survive and pay rent .

62287284
NO BATHROOM BREAKS TOMORROW

12 HOUR SHIFTS SAT AND SUN

62287347
>>62287260
so bullihs at this..

62287354
>>62287271
He literally looks like any other MF in the street

62287575
>>62286974
Cool firework show Jeffy
>didn't even leave the ground
did they forget to undo the bolts?

62287805
So now that crypto is officially dead and buried, where will all the crypto money flood into?

62287806
>>62286974
RIP BRAVE GIRLS

62287807
>>62287805
ai, after that biotech, after that real estate (2035), after that pm (2040)

62287808
>>62286974
>"spacex will be overvalued!!!"
spacex made things look easy, they have an incredible moat

62287893
>>62287284
>IF YOU WAGIES ARE EVEN A MINUTE LATE --

item.title: Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now. — 55 replies — 9.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now.

Open thread • Replies: 55 • Updated: 2026-05-29 07:14 UTC

OP

Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now. AMA law related /biz/ questions you may have.

Early replies (first 3)

62272819
>>62272812
Can i peepee in the poopoo or will i go to jail?

62272833
>>62272819
You may

62272835
>>62272812
Is it legal for a president to collab with a news outlet to pump & dump the stock market while insiders make bank on it?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62275435
>>62272812

Are you going to be an actual trial attorney with balls or be a paper pushing cuck like most?

I can't make up my mind who's intelligence the general public overrates more between doctors and lawyers.

62275484
>>62272812
What was your LSAT score? What is your school’s rank? What was your GPA? Where are you interning this summer?

How big is your pp?

62275816
>>62275176
Ass for most of my life but as I’ve gotten older, tits
>>62275435
I honestly don’t know yet, it’s only been one year and I’m not even dead set on which area of the law I want to practice in.
>>62275484
>What was your LSAT score?
170
>What is your school’s rank?
Mid 20s
>What was your GPA?
In undergrad or 1L? In undergrad it was a 3.4 in an easy major, I just didn’t give a shit in college. In 1L it was a 3.5
>Where are you interning this summer?
I’m RAing with a professor and doing a one month internship with a local judge later in the summer.

>How big is your pp?
5.8 inches massive

62275842
Do you ever consider yourself a second class profession compared to finance chads who value and trade and make money off the silly little financial obligation contracts lawyers write up?

62275952
>>62275842
Again dude I just finished my first out of three years of law school, so I don’t know what the actual practice looks like at all.

62276043
>>62275952
I'm just ribbing you buddy, a little white collar banter if you will

62277022
>>62275816
>170 LSAT

Acceptable.

>Mid 20s Law School

Acceptable.

>3.5 1L GPA

Acceptable.

>I’m RAing with a professor and doing a one month internship with a local judge later in the summer.

It’s over. You are cooked. BIG oof. Thanks for playing.

62277039
>>62277022
Why is it over? And why didn’t you address my penis size?

62277080
>>62277039
If you are not interning at an AMLAW 50 firm your 1L summer then you literally need to kill yourself.

[spoiler]My posts are intended to satirize the rampant one-upsmanship in the legal profession. I don’t actually care about your genitalia you dumb retard. It is a metaphor.[/spoiler]

62278084
>>62277080
Well don’t scare me like that anon. You freaked me out since some of the people in my class do have Big Law internships and it’s kinda tough not to feel jealous sometimes with how much they’re getting paid.

62278202
>>62278084
Funposting aside, (You) should be (mildly) freaked out because spending your 1L summer split between volunteering for a professor and a one (1) month stint with a (non-federal) jjudge is concerning. Especially if the stats (You) quoted me earlier are true.

(You) are underachieving.

62278255
>>62278202
I don't know what to tell you. I applied for a billion 1L summer positions and even got a lot of interviews but none of them panned out. It would have been nice to get something more prestigious looking but I'm just glad I finally got something lined up. i have heard also that as long as you do do something legal related 1L summer then firms don't really care what it was, is that not true?

I'm not actually aiming for Big Law or anything, I'm mostly interested in environmental and real estate law and potentially being a prosecutor. I dunno I've only taken the basic classes and it feels weird this expectation to know for certain what you want to do before you've learned anything about the field.

62278315
>>62278255
I appreciate your sincerity, and I will stop frogposting.

Forget about prestige. Focus on what will get you paid. I encourage you to network with local boutique and midsize firms. These days, Big Law internships are reserved for black lesbians (unironically). You’re doing fine.

Godspeed.

62278366
>>62278315
>Forget about prestige. Focus on what will get you paid
I am getting paid for the RA job though.
>I encourage you to network with local boutique and midsize firms
This might be self-evident as a 4chan poster but I absolutely hate networking, really suck at it, and would like to avoid it as much as possible. Is it really that essential to getting a good job? Do I just have to force myself to do it? I heard the 2L summer hiring process was supposed to be way chiller and less insane.

And thanks for your advice by the way!

62278395
>>62278366
Networking is somewhat required for any career path unfortunately. My condolences anon

62280144
>>62278395

62280365
>>62278366
You will miss out of some of the best opportunities in this profession if you don’t network, unfortunately. I only got my current job because one of my professors was willing to refer me to someone on the inside.

62280758
Are there any good legal loopholes for evading debt collection? I'm writing a story, and there's a chapter where the MC scrambles to hide his assets from the chapter 7 trustee.

62281712
>>62280758
I know several but will tell you none

62282639
>>62280758
>I'm writing a story
I dunno if I believe you there anon

62286326
>>62272812
Truebit was hacked in January you think a lawsuit has a chance?

62286469
>>62272812
How much are jews over-represented in law?

Can I get in with a science degree and mediocre GPA (3.5)? If i can't get a science job with my MS then thinking of pivoting into patent law.

62286482
what are the reputations of people who go into different types of law? like are torts people regarded as ambulance chasers? people who go into real estate considered retards? etc

62286539
how do I sue companies for discrimination during the interview process?

62286591
>>62273041
>the irish people have done nothing for humanity, and neither will you. You are niggers compared to Russians

62286708
>>62273090
What were your classmates like? Like how nursing is single moms and programming is white sexual deviants and engineering students are just a bunch of Aryan chads.

62286727
>>62272812
Kys lawfag

62286755
>>62286326
Probably not
>>62286469
Definitely overrepresented but I dunno how much.
>>62286482
I definitely know personal injury lawyers are seen as being kind of skeavy, the civil rights and public interest stereotype is they're very wealthy and privileged with their parents paying for everything but still act condescending towards everyone trying to get paid. I don't really know stereotypes for the rest.
>>62286539
You basically can't unless you have a smoking gun like emails between each other saying they were discriminating or they accidentally sent you a voicemail joking about that guy they just discriminated against
>>62286708
Probably 60% women, and 95% white other than one asian girl. There were a few different cliques but the only really shared trait was alcoholism and being nerds. Some are the friendly type of nerds and some the smug type of nerds. Like there was one guy who would go to TA office hours and when someone would ask the TA a question he would very quickly butt in and try to answer the question for the TA, then at the end ask the TA "is that basically right?" pissed people off. Most people were just normal though.
>>62286727
Why come?

62287528
I know you said it's only for /biz/ questions, but I'm really curious: do you think Jigsaw could ever be prosecuted? This is something we're regularly discussing on >>>/tv/.

62287539
>>62272812
Is gooning illegal if she doesn't notice?

item.title: is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf — 137 replies — 9.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf

Open thread • Replies: 137 • Updated: 2026-05-28 21:38 UTC

OP

is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf

Early replies (first 3)

62275893
>>62275877
Checked and ikd but it's telling how Chris/fishy/juels et al are ignoring CLPR like their lifes depend on it.

62275918
>>62275893
>CLPR
Is this the new talking point? A acronym we have never heard of is another chainlink killer?

62275930
>>62275918
yes its very 'organically' being spammed across this board right now in every thread, not being shilled directly because they know that doesn't work here (they tried one thread and got no traction). hedera shills are the least organic in the space, likely because they're mostly bought/paid for, another anon saved screen caps of their payout program for the shills. also the leemon face memes are awful, just being ugly is not enough to create a meme you fucking jeets.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283730
>>62283715
>Either Hedera "servers" interact with them ("step 1")
No the servers would be in their respective ledgers, not hedera.
> or they integrate Hedera's code into their base chain code ("step 2").
It's not "Hedera's code" whatever the fuck that means.

Two ledgers can talk with each others without having to interact with Hedera at all. Cope all you want it's just how it is.

62283741
>>62283730
>the servers would be in their respective ledgers, not hedera
They're Hedera's servers. The servers run Hedera's code.

>It's not "Hedera's code"
CLPR is coded by Hedera. It's Hedera's code.

62283782
>>62283741
Two ledgers can talk with each other via CLPR without having to interact with the Hedera DLT at all.

Keep coping jew.

62283798
>>62283782
>via CLPR without having to interact with the Hedera DLT at all.
Not talking about the DLT, I'm talking about the project.

62283808
>>62283798
So again, why are you so sure that CLPR will not be adopted at the validator level? This makes CCIP and all other bridges obsolete.

62283817
>>62283702
>>62283730
>>62283782
Why do you keep larping about the technicals when they havent been released?

62283819
>>62283808
Firstly because no other chain wants to alter their base code and functionality by injecting someone else's shit.
Secondly because it's retarded to ask blockchain validators/miners/nodes/... to moonlight as oracles.

62283824
>>62283817
Did you even read
https://hashgraph.com/blog/clpr-a-new-bridgeless-standard-for-cross-ledger-communication/
?
I know it's not in depth but it's something

62283873
>>62283824
yeah I screwed up, i meant the guy you were talking to and one other retard. the only rebuttal so far has been to throw as much bullshit at the wall as possible. i think this clpr thing is not getting the serious consideration from CLL it deserves.

62283889
>>62278023
lmao i didnt report shit lol
>>62278047
swapping ids? where faggot? i posted the op in my car, now im on wifi. made that very clear early on.

i get it you guys married your bags, but I didnt and until this is addressed by CLL im going to continue to assume there’s a serious contender until it is addressed by serg.

62283896
>>62283678
you people are so weird constantly id hopping, it gives away that you're jeets, nobody else would care that much on this dead board.
also to the other two faggots, anon engaged you seriously and you gave the game away by getting mad at him, you're not genuine link holders you're hbar baggies spamming the board. you faggots did this once before when link first joined that faggy council trying to act like friends and getting people to buy your bags. hbar is never going to be a thing here, leemon is never going to be a meme, go somewhere else (although i get you're paid to be here so it is what it is)

62283902
>>62283873
What I'm talking about is literally from the documentation.
In all cases, this system requires the introduction of external data from one chain to another.

Step 1 involves "dedicated servers", which basically means third party oracles.

Step 2 involves ledgers implementing CLPR "natively" whereby the validators become the endpoints.
Meaning the ledger's miners/nodes/validators/... have to operate as oracles.

62283908
>>62283873
>has been to throw as much bullshit at the wall as possible.
lmao that is exactly what you faggots are doing in this and like 20 other threads on the board right now. meanwhile anon actually rebuts everything and you faggots just pilpul and pretend you didn't hear previous responses.

62283922
>>62283896
>>62283908
>there can be NO questions
keep coping faggot. count my posts and suck these nuts.
>>62283902
ok then post the fucking sauce

62283946
>>62283922
>ok then post the fucking sauce
The sauce is all over this thread

62283951
>>62283922
you are an hbar baggie, you are a faggot, hbar will never ever be a thing

62284227
>>62283896
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62284232
>>62284227
>1pbtid
keep swapping

62284314
>>62283922
It seems like nobody here really knows anything about CLPR. Hell they didn't even know it existed two days ago lol.

62284575
>>62278842
Wow another private key hacked. Chainlink would have prevented this.

62284723
>>62284232
lick my ass, nigger

62284748
>>62284723
>lick my ass, nigger

62285049
>>62283946
>theres no sauce
I get it, this is a curveball, but if we lie to ourselves, like saying Chainlink is the only solution forever and ever, then we sound like xrpcels. the only thing posted as been press releases, nothing more, otherwise i wouldve read that instead of talking to low iq biz retards.
>>62284314
ikr. it’s been almost a month, wish there was more than silence. cll governs hbar they know how clpr works, wish theyd share.
>>62283951
Think what you like faggot. again, suck these hairy nuts and enjoy.

62285504
>>62285049
>>theres no sauce
You were spoonfed the sauce as a url in a direct reply. What is wrong with you.

62285523
>>62285049
>if we lie to ourselves
>we
fuck off

62285569
i'm too demoralized to read this thread. just tell me, are we going to make it?

62285584
>>62285569
CLPR just buckbroke CCIP.
But the rest of Chainlink services are fine.

62285645
>>62285584
Nobody will ever fall for the HBAR shilling, you are so fucking bad at this.

62285660
>>62285645
CLPR doesn't even benefit HBAR
Anyone can use it without using hedera

62286036
>>62285584
It didn't buckbreak anything though. Nothing changes for ccip.
You've been dumpstered several times in this thread and at this point you're clearly just a deranged subhuman repeating his nonsense for the sake of it. This is how your threads usually go when you get your new DOA talking points
>make the OP on your phone
>respond to yourself on your pc for several days
>hop ids on phone to try and back yourself up but it's really obvious while you get schooled
Just kill yourself, fuckin loser lol

item.title: If I invest $10k in this, how much will I have by EOY? — 32 replies — 7.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
If I invest $10k in this, how much will I have by EOY?

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-05-29 08:37 UTC

OP

If I invest $10k in this, how much will I have by EOY?

Early replies (first 3)

62286606
10k

62286609
8k

62286611
>>62286602
$10k

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62286614
>>62286602
$7500

62286617
>>62286602
2K

Maybe I think it would go private beiefly though so you may just get bought out for 2$ a share before it goes private again elon ah heroes and it's banrupted or bought by boeing for a dollar

62286623
And ignore emotional investing advice like >>62286617 btw

62286627
>>62286602

62286658
WELCOME TO THE GRAND COMMUNIST CIRCUS
Step right up, comrades! In today’s performance, everybody owns everything… which somehow means nobody can find toilet paper. HONK HONK.
First act: The government confiscates the bakery to “help the people.” Five minutes later the shelves looking like they got raided by raccoons. But don’t worry! A man in a giant red suit appears yelling, “Production has increased by 9000%!” while standing directly in front of an empty store. Audience applauds nervously.
Second act: The Amazing Disappearing Freedom Trick! One second you say, “Hey maybe this system ain’t working,” and POOF — now you taking an unscheduled vacation to a mysterious work camp somewhere cold enough to freeze soup solid. Incredible! Absolute cinema.
Third act: Equality for all! Everybody gets the same tiny apartment, the same sad bread loaf, and the same mandatory portrait of Supreme Mustache Leader smiling at you from every wall like a haunted mascot. Meanwhile the party elites eating steak backstage talking about “shared sacrifice.” HONK HONK HONK.
And the economy? Brother, the economy riding a unicycle on fire juggling potatoes. Every problem gets solved with another five-year plan written by twelve dudes who never touched a shovel in their life.
Communism really be like:“We fixed greed!”creates a bigger greedy government
Peak clown technology.

62286790
>>62286735
Yo, SpaceX out here moving like that one dude in class who borrows money for lunch then flexes fake chains on Instagram. They screaming “future of humanity” while burning billions like it’s a mixtape launch party. Bro got rockets exploding on livestream so often people think it’s a Netflix trailer.

Meanwhile the finance students gotta calculate ROI, cash flow, and risk management while SpaceX be treating investor money like GTA cheat codes. “Yeah bro, we lost another rocket… but next one gonna be fire.” Literally.

Their whole business model sounds like a group project carried by government contracts. NASA basically the sugar daddy keeping the lights on while Elon tweets memes at 3 AM like he’s avoiding accounting homework. Imagine telling your professor: “My company valuation is vibes and Mars dreams.” You’d get escorted out the lecture hall instantly.

And don’t even start on the Mars talk. My guy can’t get reliable Wi-Fi on Earth but wants people paying rent on another planet. Finance professors teaching discounted cash flow while SpaceX valuation is discounted reality flow. Every launch got people acting like it’s the second coming of Einstein, then boom — another stainless steel bottle rocket doing cartwheels over Texas.

Street truth? SpaceX is that rich kid who crashes daddy’s Lambo, buys another one, and somehow gets called a genius entrepreneur. In any normal finance case study, half these decisions would be labeled “high-risk speculative behavior with catastrophic downside exposure.” But slap a cool rocket edit on TikTok and suddenly everybody forgets basic economics.

At this point the only thing reaching orbit consistently is the hype.
